title: polygala amara subsp. alpina
intro: Polygala amara subsp. alpina, also known as Alpine Milkwort, is a perennial herb belonging to the Polygalaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, as it contains saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are known to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antitussive properties. The health benefits of this plant include soothing coughs, reducing inflammation, and easing respiratory congestion, making it a popular remedy for conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Alpine Milkwort can be consumed in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
